titleStmt.title: SII 25.37: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 37. (A.R. No. 37 of 1908.) TIRUPPARAṄKUṈṞAM, MADURAI TALUK, MADURAI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On one of the pillars in the rock-cut cave.

This Vaṭṭeḻuttu inscription is engraved in archaic characters of 8th century A.D has been attributed to Jaṭila Parāntaka of the Āṉaimalai inscription dated Kali 3871 (see S.I.I., Vol. XIV, Nos 1 and 2, Ep. Ind., Vol. VIII, pp. 317 ff).

It records the construction of the Tirukkōyil (shrine) and of the Śrītaṭākam (tank) by Śāttaṉ Gaṇapati, a resident of Karavandapura who is known as Pāṇḍi-Amṛitamaṅgalavaraiyaṉ, and is stated to have belonged to the Vaidya family and to have been the mahāsāmanta of the king. The shrines of Durgādēvī and Jyēshṭhādēvī were constructed by Nakkaṉ-koṟṟi, who is described as the Dharmapatni, probably of this mahāsāmanta.

This general who was also called Sāmanta Bhīma and an important member of the Vaidya family (vai(j)ya-mukhya) is stated to have excavated a temple for Śaṁbhu and to have consecreated the image of the god in the Kali year 3874 (773 A.D.). If this Śiva temple referred to in an inscription from Tirupparaṅkuṉṟam (A.R.Ep., 1951-52, No. B. 143) and the tirukkōyil (temple) stated to have been excavated by Śattaṉ Gaṇapati are identical, as it is not unlikely, the date of the latter event, viz., the 6th year of Māṟañjaḍaiyaṉ, must be equated with 773 A.D., the date of the former event. This gives us the date of his accession as 768 A.D.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. XIV, No. 3.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī komāṟañ caṭaiyaṟku
rājyaparṣam Āṟāvatu cellā
niṟpa maṟṟavaṟku mahā
sāman=taṉākiya karavan=tapurādhi
vāsī vaijyan pāṇṭi Ami
rtamaṅkalavaraiyaṉāi
ṉa cāttaṅ kaṇapati ti
ruttu vittatu tirukkoi
lum śrītaṭākamum Itaṉuḷa
ṟa muḷḷatum . maṟṟava
ṟku dharmmapatni Ākiya na
kkaṅ koṟṟiyāṟ ceya
p paṭṭatu durgādevi ko
iluñ jeṣṭai koilum .
Digital edition of SII 25.37 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
